Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

I don't think this is needed for master.  This was only needed when the block_maps stats was added to an old branch.

I think this patch should just be abandoned.

Patch Set 1:

The collectd parsing is incredibly fragile, since it was originally designed for "one value per file" output, and brw_stats is nothing like that.

Essentially, it is like a single giant regexp for the entire stats file, so if there is some new line in the middle of the stats output then the regexp is broken and doesn't match anything.

However, lustrefs_collector has already been updated to handle this new format (AFAIK), and uses a more robust parsing mechanism to avoid similar breakage in the future.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Passed enforced test review-ldiskfs-dne on RHEL 9.3/x86_64 uploaded by Onyx Autotest 2 from onyx-25vm7: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/feae8a8c-359c-4013-8265-d3baf16d1def ran 5 tests.

parents.subject: LU-18110 doc: llapi_pcc_detach_fid_fd wrong enum names

commit.subject: LU-15564 osd-ldiskfs: hide alloc time in brw_stats
commit.message:
LU-15564 osd-ldiskfs: hide alloc time in brw_stats

Do not show the "block maps msec" stats in brw_stats by default
as this breaks collectd and lustrefs_collector parsing.

Add an "enable_brw_block_maps" parameter that can be used to
disable the display of this statistic (it is always collected).

Enable the "enable_stats_header" parameter automatically in the
same way.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Fixes: f97ce54357 ("LU-15564 osd: add allocation time histogram")
Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: Ib5e33bd98085aaf4a5a5d39283d5d334b93ebbe5
